tool.name: 3Dtrees: RayCloudTools
tool.command: mkdir -p 'in' 'out' 'out_segmented_laz' 'out_tree_model_txt' 'out_tree_info_txt' 'out_tree_mesh_ply' 'out_terrain_mesh_ply' && #for $f in $input #set $input_name = str($f.element_identifier) ln -sf '$f' 'in/${input_name}.${f.ext}' && python3 -u /src/run.py --dataset-path 'in/${input_name}.${f.ext}' --output-dir 'out' --gradient '$gradient' --max-diameter '$max_diameter' --crop-length '$crop_length' --distance-limit '$distance_limit' --height-min '$height_min' --girth-height-ratio '$girth_height_ratio' --global-taper '$global_taper' --global-taper-factor '$global_taper_factor' --gravity-factor '$gravity_factor' $branch_segmentation $treeinfo $branch_data && ## Some RayCloudTools artifacts can be absent depending on the chosen parameters ## and what the backend emits for a given input, so stage outputs only if they exist. mv "out/${input_name}_segmented.laz" "out_segmented_laz/${input_name}_segmented.laz" && if [ -f "out/${input_name}_trees_mesh.ply" ]; then mv "out/${input_name}_trees_mesh.ply" "out_tree_mesh_ply/${input_name}_trees_mesh.ply"; fi && if [ -f "out/${input_name}_mesh.ply" ]; then mv "out/${input_name}_mesh.ply" "out_terrain_mesh_ply/${input_name}_mesh.ply"; fi && rm -f 'in/${input_name}.${f.ext}' && #if $treeinfo if [ -f "out/${input_name}_trees_info.txt" ]; then mv "out/${input_name}_trees_info.txt" "out_tree_info_txt/${input_name}_trees_info.txt"; fi && #else if [ -f "out/${input_name}_trees.txt" ]; then mv "out/${input_name}_trees.txt" "out_tree_model_txt/${input_name}_trees.txt"; fi && #end if #end for true

param.name: input
param.label: Input Dataset Collection (.laz)
param.help: Input list collection of LAZ files to process in one RayCloudTools run.

param.label: Maximum diameter
param.help: maximum trunk diameter in segmenting trees (m)

param.label: Crop length
param.help: Crops branches up to this distance from tip (m).

param.label: Distance limit
param.help: Maximum distance between neighbour points in a tree (m)

param.label: Minimum tree height
param.help: Minimum height counted as a tree (m)

param.label: Girth height ratio
param.help: Amount up tree's height to estimate trunk girth

param.label: Global taper
param.help: Force taper value (diameter per length) for trees under global_taper_factor of max tree height - use 0 to estimate global taper from the data

param.label: Global taper factor
param.help: 1 estimates same taper for whole scan, 0 is per-tree tapering, values between 0 and 1 produce soft cutoff at this amount of max tree height

param.label: Branch segmentation
param.help: Segments per branch segment - assigns IDs per branch segment instead of per tree instance

param.label: Treeinfo
param.help: Calculate tree attributes - dbh, height, crown radius, volume, ...

param.label: Branch data
param.help: Calculate branch attributes - branch number, segment_length, branch order number, extension and position in branch integers per-segment

tool.help:
**What it does**
This tool performs graph-based tree instance segmentation on LiDAR point clouds using the **RayExtract** algorithm (Devereux et al., 2026) implemented as part of **RayCloudTools** (Lowe & Stepanas, 2021). It is platform-agnostic and works across airborne (ULS), terrestrial (TLS), and mobile (MLS) laser scanning data, as long as trees are well-represented by points along the entire tree length.
-----
**Processing Steps**
- Convert input point cloud to LAZ 1.2 - Import point cloud as ray cloud - Extract terrain - Extract trees - Convert point color (segmentation result) to instance IDs - Add instance IDs back to input point cloud - Optional: Calculate tree attributes (dbh, height, crown radius, volume, ...)
-----
**Output**
One collection per artifact type:
- **Segmented point clouds (LAZ):** `{basename}_segmented.laz` (instance dimension `PredInstance_RCT`) - **Tree models (TXT):** `{basename}_trees.txt` - **Tree info (TXT):** `{basename}_trees_info.txt` when tree attributes are enabled - **Tree meshes (PLY):** `{basename}_trees_mesh.ply` - **Terrain meshes (PLY):** `{basename}_mesh.ply`
The input is a single Galaxy list collection, and the wrapper iterates over that collection internally. That keeps the outputs flat as one collection per artifact type instead of nested list collections.
Some artifact collections may be empty for a given run. That depends on the selected parameters, whether `treeinfo` is enabled, and which files RayCloudTools actually emits for each input tile.
*`{basename}` is the input dataset name without its last extension (for example `tile_01` from `tile_01.laz`). Tile identifiers are preserved as-is, so names such as `c00r00` or `c00_r00` stay unchanged in the outputs.*
***Tip:** We recommend using the '3DTrees: Tile and Merge' or '3DTrees: Smart Tile' tool to process large point clouds (workflow: tile → segment → merge)!*
-----
**Third-Party Components**
You agree to comply with the licence terms for these components as part of accessing the tool.
**RayCloudTools:** Copyright (c) 2020, Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation (CSIRO) ABN 41 687 119 230 | [CSIRO-Open Source Software License (variation of the BSD / MIT License)](https://github.com/csiro-robotics/raycloudtools); **PDAL:** Copyright (c) 2025, Hobu, Inc. (howard@hobu.co) | [pdal License (BSD)](https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL?tab=License-1-ov-file#readme); **NumPy:** Copyright (c) 2005-2025, NumPy Developers | [numpy License (BSD)](https://github.com/numpy/numpy?tab=License-1-ov-file#readme); **LasPy:** Copyright (c) 2012, Grant Brown, grant.brown73 at gmail.com, Copyright (c) 2012, Howard Butler, hobu.inc at gmail.com, Copyright (c) 2020, Thomas Montaigu, thomas.montaigu at laposte.net | [laspy License](https://github.com/laspy/laspy?tab=License-1-ov-file#readme); **Open3D:** Copyright (c) 2018-2023 www.open3d.org | [Open3D License (MIT)](https://github.com/isl-org/Open3D?tab=License-1-ov-file#readme).
